issues.description: ProfilePage component lacks dedicated unit tests. While ProfileForm and profileStore have test coverage for edit mode, the ProfilePage component itself (as implemented in App.tsx) doesn't have specific test cases verifying the conditional rendering between display and edit modes.

The edit form pre-fills values from the profile store at render time. If the profile were to change in the store while the form is open (unlikely in practice), the form would not reflect the new values. This is a minor edge case.

sharedStateObservations.description: ProfilePage edit mode implementation follows the same pattern as other store-backed components - checking store state and conditionally rendering. The profileStore properly resets isEditing on all state transitions (save, cancel, clear).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Lines 83-116: ProfilePage uses useProfileStore hook, checks isEditing state, and conditionally renders ProfileForm or static display
